query: effect of betamethasone on neonatal bilirubin levels in preterm infants

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Treatment / Management -- Corticosteroids
retrieved_docs.content: When given, 1 of 2 antenatal corticosteroid regiments should be used as follows: Two doses of betamethasone 12 mg intramuscularly (IM), given 24 hours apart Four doses of dexamethasone 6 mg IM, given every 12 hours [1] [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Treatment / Management -- Corticosteroids
retrieved_docs.content: The 2016 Antenatal Late Preterm Steroids (ALPS) trial was a double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized controlled trial that studied the effect of antenatal betamethasone in 2831 patients with a singleton pregnancy between 34 and 36 6/7 weeks EGA at high risk of PTB within the next 7 days and before 37 weeks EGA who had not previously received steroids. [34] This study's results showed that antenatal betamethasone significantly reduced the risk of neonatal respiratory morbidity in the study population. However, a secondary analysis of this trial found that those with sPTL or PPROM had lower rates of neonatal respiratory complications when compared to those with indicated PTB, irrespective of betamethasone exposure in the late preterm period. [35] Antenatal corticosteroids also significantly increase the risk of neonatal hypoglycemia. [34]

retrieved_docs.title: Routine Newborn Care -- Issues of Concern -- Newborn Care
retrieved_docs.content: Hyperbilirubinemia is common in newborns, and most infants have some visible jaundice in the first week after birth. Jaundice during the first 24 hours of life is pathologic and requires investigation. Jaundice may be due to an immature uridine diphosphate glucuronyltransferase enzyme in the liver, elevated hemoglobin levels in newborns and increased destruction of RBCs. Bilirubin screening is done before hospital discharge either transcutaneous or measuring serum levels. [1]

retrieved_docs.content: ACOG and WHO recommend considering a single repeat course of antenatal corticosteroids (ie, "rescue steroids") in individuals who have received 1 previous course of steroids at least 7 to 14 days prior and are currently. A rescue course may consist of either a standard 48-hour regimen described above or a single dose of betamethasone, which evidence suggests is also effective after initial standard therapy. [42]

retrieved_docs.content: Within 1 hour of birth, intramuscular vitamin K should be administered. Prophylaxis with erythromycin ointment to both eyes and injection of hepatitis B vaccine are recommendations, while the baby has skin-to-skin with the mother. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Tocolysis -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Tocolysis is clinically significant because it increases the amount of time to perform neonatal interventions. The WHO has shown that it is beneficial to delay delivery by up to 48 hours, allowing for antenatal corticosteroids to help fetal lung maturity in premature neonates. [28] There have been publications that have questioned if tocolytics have benefits on major neonatal morbidity. [29] [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Neonatal Jaundice -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify pathologic jaundice and differentiate it from physiologic jaundice. Delineate the etiologies of neonatal jaundice. Implement evidence-based management options for neonatal jaundice. Identify how the interprofessional team can work collaboratively to prevent the potentially profound complications of neonatal jaundice by applying knowledge about its prevention, presentation, evaluation, and management. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Budesonide -- Indications -- Croup
retrieved_docs.content: In very low birth weight infants, intratracheal administration of budesonide-surfactant combination results in reduced incidence of bronchopulmonary dysplasia (BPD) or composite outcome of BPD or death. [17] Besides, early initiation of treatment with 1 mg of budesonide inhalation suspension appears to reduce overall corticosteroid usage and the need for mechanical ventilation in premature, very low birth weight babies at risk for chronic pulmonary disease. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Neonatal Jaundice -- Treatment / Management -- Phototherapy
retrieved_docs.content: However, for premature infants, few standardized guidelines exist, and most hospitals compile their own protocols for phototherapy and exchange transfusion in preterm infants based on birth weight and gestational age. [31] The efficacy of phototherapy depends on the dose and wavelength of light used, as well as the infant's exposed surface area. Increasing the dose of PT can be achieved by placing phototherapy units at a minimum safe distance from the infant and increasing the number of units used.

retrieved_docs.title: Periventricular and Intraventricular Hemorrhage -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: The primary treatment strategy should be aimed at the prevention of preterm birth, if possible. This should include the routine administration of antenatal corticosteroids if preterm birth is expected and the transfer of the mother to a facility that has advanced capabilities of caring for very low birth weight infants before delivery. Maternal corticosteroid administration for fetal lung maturation showed a protective effect against PIVH in preterm newborns. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Abnormal Neonatal EEG -- Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Recently, actigraphy at a medium threshold has been useful in illustrating sleep among typically developing preterm infants in the NICU; thereby, aEEG may be an adjunct to actigraphy in evaluating sleep/wake states in the NICU setting. [25] Another study assessed the effect of maternal betamethasone administration at 34 to 35 weeks of accelerated neonatal amplitude-integrated EEG (aEEG) maturation. Findings revealed that betamethasone resulted in changes in lower border voltage support accelerated electrical activity. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Neonatal Jaundice -- Treatment / Management -- Treatment of Unconjugated Hyperbilirubinemia
retrieved_docs.content: Phototherapy and exchange transfusion are the mainstays of treatment for newborns with unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia. Other treatment modalities may be utilized based on patient response to these therapies. The approach to treatment is determined by the neurotoxicity risk factors present, neonatal gestational age, and hour-specific TSB. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Treatment / Management -- Corticosteroids
retrieved_docs.content: Antenatal corticosteroids improve neonatal outcomes when given to individuals at high risk of PTB; these are most effective between 48 hours and 7 days before PTB. [2] For these reasons, antenatal corticosteroids are universally recommended for patients at risk of PTB between viability and 34 weeks EGA. ACOG, the Society for Maternal-Fetal Medicine (SMFM), and the WAPM-PMF guidelines recommend considering antenatal corticosteroids in the periviable period, down to 22 0/7 weeks if neonatal resuscitation is planned. [2] However, beyond 34 weeks, recommendations vary across professional organizations.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Treatment / Management -- Corticosteroids
retrieved_docs.content: WAPM-PMF recommends individualizing treatment and suggests a 34 0/7 weeks EGA cutoff for treatment with antenatal corticosteroids. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Neonatal Respiratory Distress Syndrome -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: The beneficial effect of antenatal corticosteroid use after 34 weeks of gestation is controversial due to the lack of information in long term developmental outcomes. [44] Maternal antenatal corticosteroids are recommended for possible preterm delivery in the next seven days between 23rd and 34th week gestational age. [45] Some institutions offer antenatal corticosteroids at 22 weeks if anticipating delivery within the next week. Despite multiple interventions targeting various etiologies, the goal of preventing preterm birth remains elusive.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 2268
retrieved_docs.content: The neonatal qualifier value encompasses several newborn conditions. It includes umbilical bleeding after birth, which can occur when a loose umbilical cord clamp leads to bleeding. It also occurs in various forms of neonatal jaundice, such as breast‑milk jaundice, physiologic jaundice that results from immature liver function in fetuses or newborns, and jaundice that is linked to premature delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 2 -- Community 1081
retrieved_docs.content: NEONATAL HEMORRHAGE AND HEMOLYTIC DISEASE OF THE NEWBORN is a complex neonatal condition that gives rise to several related complications. One important manifestation is neonatal jaundice that results from delayed conjugation due to the immature development of the hepatic conjugating system. In addition, the condition includes نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ (neonatal respiratory tract hemorrhage), which reflects the hemorrhagic component of the disease. Various forms of neonatal jaundice are also linked to this disorder: يرقان نووي (neonatal jaundice) in general, يرقان نووي ناجم عن التمنيع الإسوي لدى الأجنة أو المواليد (neonatal jaundice caused by hemolysis of the fetus or newborn), and يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان (jaundice associated with preterm birth). Together, these interrelated entities illustrate how hemorrhagic and hemolytic processes in the newborn can impair liver function, lead to delayed bilirubin conjugation, and produce respiratory bleeding, underscoring the need for comprehensive assessment and coordinated management of affected infants.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 0 -- Community 18
retrieved_docs.content: and perinatal jaundice are evaluated by total hemoglobin concentration, and laboratory values below reference ranges support their diagnosis. Neonatal jaundice manifests in multiple specific forms: neonatal jaundice due to delayed conjugation, neonatal jaundice due to delayed conjugation from delayed development of the conjugating system, breast‑milk jaundice (يرقان حليب الثدي), nuclear jaundice (يرقان نووي), nuclear jaundice caused by epidermal immaturity in the fetus or newborn (يرقان نووي ناجم عن التمنيع الإسوي لدى الأجنة أو المواليد), physiologic neonatal jaundice (يرقان وليدي) and physiologic neonatal jaundice associated with preterm birth (يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان). These jaundice types arise from hemolysis, delayed bilirubin conjugation, nutritional factors (breast‑milk) or birth‑timing complications, and they frequently co‑occur with hemorrhagic events. Hemorrhagic disorders anchored in the Neonatal period include loss of umbilical cord clamp leading to bleeding

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 1097
retrieved_docs.content: Neonatal (qualifier value) is associated with a loose umbilical cord clamp that can lead to bleeding, and it occurs in several neonatal conditions including neonatal respiratory tract hemorrhage (نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ). It is also found in neonatal jaundice (يرقان نووي), particularly the physiologic form that results from immature bilirubin metabolism in fetuses or newborns (يرقان نووي ناجم عن التمنيع الإسوي لدى الأجنة أو المواليد), as well as in jaundice that is linked to preterm birth (يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان). These interconnections highlight the range of complications that can be present in the neonatal period and underscore the importance of careful monitoring and management of umbilical cord handling, respiratory health, and bilirubin levels in newborns.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1138
retrieved_docs.content: Lucey‑Driscoll syndrome (disorder) is classified as a neonatal condition, indicating that it manifests during the newborn period. The neonatal (qualifier value) stage is also the context in which several specific clinical entities occur, including نزف سري بعد الولادة (umbilical bleeding after birth), نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ (neonatal respiratory tract bleeding), يرقان حليب الثدي (breast‑milk jaundice), and يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان (premature‑related neonatal jaundice). Recognizing that these entities all occur in the neonatal timeframe helps clinicians understand the spectrum of disorders and presentations that may be present in newborns, including those affected by Lucey‑Driscoll syndrome, and guides appropriate evaluation and management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 2 -- Community 1163
retrieved_docs.content: The qualifier “Neonatal (qualifier value)” designates conditions that manifest during the newborn period. It is applied to the occurrence of نزف سري بعد الولادة (umbilical bleeding after birth), نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ (neonatal respiratory tract bleeding), يرقان حليب الثدي (breast‑milk jaundice), يرقان نووي (physiologic neonatal jaundice), and يرقان نووي ناجم عن التمنيع الإسوي لدى الأجنة أو المواليد (physiologic jaundice resulting from immature hepatic conjugation in fetuses or newborns). Recognizing these entities as neonatal helps clinicians focus assessment and management on disorders specific to the early post‑natal timeframe.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 854
retrieved_docs.content: Neonatal hemorrhage and hemolytic disease of the newborn encompasses a range of related clinical findings. It includes neonatal jaundice due to delayed conjugation, reflecting impaired bilirubin processing in the early infant. The condition is also linked to bleeding from the umbilical cord, described in Dutch as “losgeraakte navelstrengklem; bloeding,” and to post‑delivery umbilical bleeding, expressed in Arabic as “نزف سري بعد الولادة.” Additional manifestations involve bleeding within the neonatal respiratory tract, noted in Arabic as “نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ.” Finally, the disease is associated with jaundice that is specifically related to preterm birth, described in Arabic as “يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان.” Together, these interconnected signs underscore the importance of vigilant assessment for both hemorrhagic events and bilirubin‑related complications in newborns affected by this condition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2323
retrieved_docs.content: Bacterial sepsis of the newborn (unspecified) is a neonatal condition that, together with a range of other neonatal disorders, defines the health challenges encountered during the neonatal period. Neonatal jaundice due to delayed conjugation, postpartum hemorrhage (نزف سري بعد الولادة), neonatal respiratory tract hemorrhage (نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ), breast‑milk jaundice (يرقان حليب الثدي), and jaundice associated with preterm birth (يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان) all occur within the neonatal timeframe. Recognizing that these entities—bacterial sepsis of the newborn, delayed‑conjugation jaundice, postpartum hemorrhage, respiratory tract hemorrhage, breast‑milk‑induced jaundice, and prematurity‑related jaundice—are interrelated manifestations of neonatal physiology and pathology is essential for accurate diagnosis, timely intervention, and optimal neonatal patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2862
retrieved_docs.content: Early or threatened labor is classified as تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر, indicating a condition in which signs of labor appear prematurely and the situation is identified as threatened preterm labor, a designation that guides clinical monitoring and intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 869
retrieved_docs.content: Neonatal (qualifier value) serves as a broad category that includes several distinct neonatal conditions. Within this category, neonatal jaundice due to delayed conjugation is recognized as a specific form of jaundice that manifests in the newborn period. Additional conditions that occur in the neonatal timeframe include نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ (neonatal respiratory tract hemorrhage), يرقان حليب الثدي (breast‑milk jaundice), يرقان نووي (physiologic or nuclear jaundice), and يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان (prematurity‑related neonatal jaundice). Together, these entities illustrate the variety of etiologies—ranging from delayed bilirubin conjugation and breast‑milk factors to prematurity and respiratory bleeding—that can affect newborns, underscoring the importance of recognizing each specific presentation for accurate diagnosis and appropriate neonatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 1707
retrieved_docs.content: Neonatal disorder (اضطراب لدى حديث الولادة) is a broad category of conditions affecting newborns. Respiratory distress syndrome is identified as a specific type of neonatal disorder, and the category also includes the child conditions fetal erythroblastosis (كثرة الأرومات الحمر الجنينية) and premature rupture of membranes (رضح الولادة). Prevention and control measures are applicable to the overall neonatal disorder as well as to each of its constituent conditions—respiratory distress syndrome, fetal erythroblastosis, and premature rupture of membranes—underscoring the importance of proactive management in newborn care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2344
retrieved_docs.content: Lucey‑Driscoll syndrome (disorder) is categorized within the broader condition of neonatal hemorrhage and hemolytic disease of the newborn. Neonatal hemorrhage and hemolytic disease of the newborn encompasses several related pediatric entities, including neonatal jaundice due to delayed conjugation from delayed development of the conjugating system, نَزْف المسالك التنفسية الوَليدِيّ (neonatal respiratory tract hemorrhage), يرقان حليب الثدي (breast‑milk jaundice), and يرقان وليدي مرتبط بالولادة قبل الأوان (neonatal jaundice associated with preterm birth). These interconnected disorders illustrate the diverse mechanisms—such as impaired bilirubin conjugation, respiratory tract bleeding, milk‑related bilirubin excess, and prematurity‑related metabolic immaturity—that can contribute to hemorrhagic and hemolytic presentations in the newborn, underscoring the need for comprehensive evaluation and targeted management in this vulnerable population.
